observances.name: Ekadashi
observances.description: Fast (avoid grains). Recite Vishnu Sahasranama or Om Namo Narayanaya 108×. Read a chapter of Gita. Avoid late-night activities.

observances.name: Pradosham
observances.description: Twilight puja around local sunset. Recite Om Namah Shivaya 108×. Offer milk or bilva leaves if possible. Light a lamp facing west.

verse.devanagari: या कुन्देन्दुतुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता। या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना॥ सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा॥
verse.transliteration: yā kundendu tuṣārahāra dhavalā yā śubhravstrāvṛtā | yā vīṇāvaradaṇḍa maṇḍitakarā yā śvetapadmāsanā || sā māṃ pātu sarasvatī bhagavatī niḥśeṣajāḍyāpahā ||
verse.meaning: She who is white as jasmine, the moon, and a garland of snow; clad in pure white garments; whose hands are adorned with the veena and the boon-gesture; seated on a white lotus — may that blessed Saraswati protect me and remove all inertia.

verse.devanagari: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते। तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
verse.transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate | teṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yogakṣemaṃ vahāmy aham ||
verse.meaning: To those who worship Me alone, thinking of no other, to those ever-united in thought, I secure what they lack and preserve what they have.

verse.transliteration: mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ | āgamāpāyino 'nityās tāṃs titikṣasva bhārata ||
verse.meaning: The contact of the senses with their objects gives rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain. They are transient — endure them bravely.

verse.devanagari: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज। अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
verse.transliteration: sarvadharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja | ahaṃ tvā sarvapāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||
verse.meaning: Abandon all varieties of dharma and simply surrender unto Me alone. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions — do not fear.

verse.devanagari: ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि। धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
verse.transliteration: oṃ bhūr bhuvaḥ svaḥ tat savitur vareṇyaṃ bhargo devasya dhīmahi | dhiyo yo naḥ pracodayāt ||
verse.meaning: We meditate on the glorious splendor of the divine Savitri (Sun). May that supreme light illuminate our intellect.

verse.devanagari: नागेन्द्रहाराय त्रिलोचनाय भस्माङ्गरागाय महेश्वराय। नित्याय शुद्धाय दिगम्बराय तस्मै नकाराय नमः शिवाय॥
verse.transliteration: nāgendrahārāya trilocanāya bhasmāṅgarāgāya maheśvarāya | nityāya śuddhāya digambarāya tasmai nakārāya namaḥ śivāya ||
verse.meaning: Salutations to Shiva, who wears the king of serpents as a garland, who has three eyes, whose body is smeared with sacred ash, who is eternal, pure, and the great lord.

verse.devanagari: देवताभ्यः पितृभ्यश्च महायोगिभ्य एव च। नमः स्वधायै स्वाहायै नित्यमेव नमो नमः॥
verse.transliteration: devatābhyaḥ pitṛbhyaś ca mahāyogibhya eva ca | namaḥ svadhāyai svāhāyai nityam eva namo namaḥ ||
verse.meaning: Salutations to the gods, to the ancestors, and to the great yogis. Obeisance to Svadha and Svaha — eternal salutations again and again.

verse_of_week.devanagari: या कुन्देन्दुतुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता। या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना॥ सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा॥
verse_of_week.transliteration: yā kundendu tuṣārahāra dhavalā yā śubhravstrāvṛtā | yā vīṇāvaradaṇḍa maṇḍitakarā yā śvetapadmāsanā || sā māṃ pātu sarasvatī bhagavatī niḥśeṣajāḍyāpahā ||
verse_of_week.meaning: She who is white as jasmine, the moon, and a garland of snow; clad in pure white garments; whose hands are adorned with the veena and the boon-gesture; seated on a white lotus — may that blessed Saraswati protect me and remove all inertia.

lifestyle_recommendations: Recite Lakshmi Ashtakam or Devi mantra. Keep the home clean and fragrant.
Recite Hanuman Chalisa or a Shiva mantra. Donate sesame / mustard oil. Serve someone in need.
Recite Gayatri Mantra 108× at sunrise. Offer water to the Sun (Surya Arghya). Eat lightly.
Fast (avoid grains). Recite Vishnu Sahasranama or Om Namo Narayanaya 108×. Read a chapter of Gita. Avoid late-night activities.
Recite Ganesha shloka before any important work. Avoid starting new ventures. Be patient.
